calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #34, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-17-2013 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #34, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-11-2013 Continued To Dec-17-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dennis McQuaid and William Hoehler for Trustees. Sara Wright from Hanson Bridgett LLP also present.

The Court expressed concern about all fees (Morgan Stanley, property management, accountant, etc.) at $301,000.00, stated that $129,000.00 was distributed to the Beneficiaries, that what is filed ought to be served on all three of the Beneficiaries and the Trustee, that it ought to be verified by the Trustee, that there is substantial income coming out of the Trust and not much going to the Beneficiaries, that this is not balanced and questioned what the Trustees are being paid for/what they are doing. Counsel explained services provided and addressed the above issues. The accounting petition was continued to December 17, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for proof of service that the Beneficiaries have received notice of the fees that are being paid and for verification from all three Trustees. In order to get the tax break, Counsel can get Julie, Michael and Kirsten to waive 30-day notice.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #34, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-16-2013 Continued To Dec-11-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: William Hoehler for all three Trustees. Sara Wright (Paralegal) also present.

Mr. Hoehler explained that PEC is a real estate management entity, and that it has a holding agreement but no patnership agreement. The Court inquired as to the amounts of professional fees, legal fees, property management fees, administration expenses, etc. being paid out and questioned what services/work is being provided/performed by those Parties receiving said fees. The Court also stated that it does not see how the Beneficiaries of the Trust are benefitting when there is a whole lot more in fees going out than the Beneficiaries are earning. The accounting petition was continued to December 11, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to file a response to the Courts inquiries. Said response is to be filed by November 27, 2013.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #33, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-17-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: William Hoehler for Trustees.

The Court stated that it reviewed the papers, that it was prepared to approve the accounting and the Trustee's fee request, but that it was taking the Attorney's fees under submission. Mr. Hoehler explained that they did not bill for part of the 32nd Account until this account ($17,026.00 had not been billed), that they were billing $21,000.00 for the 33rd Account and $8,000.00 for the special project of simplifying the accounts (working on having all rental income go into the management account and then into each Trust). The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Santiago.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #29, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-07-2008 Continued To Jul-28-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dennis McQuaid for Theodore Kolb.

Mr. McQuaid stated that he would be filing the Substitution of Attorney today. The Court stated that today's proceeding was subject to the filing of the Substitution of Attorney. Mr. Taylor stated that he provided all responses to the Examiner's notes. The Court questioned if First Republic Bank would accept allowance of the accounting from the Court rather than approval. Mr. McQuaid stated that he preferred the accounting be approved as it was consistant with all the other accountings. Mr. Kolb stated that the trust did not own 100% of the property, that it was owned by the individual beneficiaries, and that last year, the Court stated that it did not want schedule E. The Court set a status date for the filing of the Substitution of Attorney for July 28, 2008. The accounting was also continued to July 28, 2008 and will be taken under submission as of that date. No appearance is required if the Substitution of Attorney is filed.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.
